title: Normalized relational schema with keys, indexes, and safe migrations
when: Designing the data layer for any app that stores related records
principle: Normalize to avoid duplication; every table has a primary key; foreign keys enforce relationships with ON DELETE rules; index the columns you filter/join on; ship idempotent migrations (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS) and never destructive ones without a guard. Use parameterized queries (never string-concat user input).
exemplar: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
created_at    TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS orders (
user_id   BIGINT NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_orders_user ON orders(user_id);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_orders_status ON orders(status);
-- query (parameterized): SELECT * FROM orders WHERE user_id = $1 AND status = $2;
pitfalls: No primary key, no indexes on join/filter columns, string-concatenated SQL (injection), destructive migrations without IF EXISTS guards.
